evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` imports UI toast (`@/shared/components/ui/toast`) and is used by non-UI hooks (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`), coupling domain/error handling to presentation.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` performs eager singleton creation at import (`export const supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient()`), mutates globals (`window.__supabase_client__`, `window.supabase`), and runs runtime initialization/instrumentation during module load, increasing import-order sensitivity.

`src/types/shots.ts` remains a broad re-export facade for `@/types/generationAndShots` while active code in assigned scope still imports it (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`), preserving an extra compatibility boundary.
`useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` explicitly documents itself as a MediaGallery-specific replacement ('Now: Just use this hook in MediaGallery'), but it is placed in global `src/shared/hooks/`.
Usage scan shows `useAdjacentPagePreloader` is imported by `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryGrid.tsx`, reinforcing single-subdomain ownership despite shared placement.

`useApiKeys.ts` couples UI hook code to Supabase auth/user table persistence (`supabase.auth.getUser`, `from('users').upsert`) while living beside unrelated hooks, weakening top-level ownership clarity.
`useAIInteractionService.ts` is consumed by prompt/image-generation flows (`PromptEditorModal`, `useImageGenForm`) but is packaged as generic shared infrastructure rather than feature-owned behavior.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts defines a 100+ field `UseImageEditValueParams` bag and mostly re-exports those fields unchanged into `ImageEditState` via `build*` pass-through builders.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts constructs and forwards large argument bags into `useInpainting`, `useMagicEditMode`, `useRepositionMode`, `useImg2ImgMode`, and then re-wraps the same values into nested return objects.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts repeats the same shared fields (`isVideo`, `readOnly`, `isSpecialEditMode`, `selectedProjectId`, `isCloudMode`) across four builder functions instead of using a narrower shared contract.

src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts and src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts each define their own `TaskDbRow` and DB->Task mapper (`mapDbTaskToTask` vs `mapDbTaskRowToTask`), so boundary translation is duplicated across seams.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx and src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx both construct near-identical `useLightboxWorkflowProps` payloads (`panel`, `shotWorkflow`, `actions`, `buttonGroupProps`), indicating repeated orchestration glue.

src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts contains both polling policy and toast UI behavior (`toast.error`) while consumers like src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts call it from data-fetch paths, coupling transport policy to presentation effects.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts wraps simple object assembly in `useMemo` but depends on `[params]`, so memoization is usually invalidated each render while retaining complexity.
src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts does the same `useMemo(..., [params])` pattern around deterministic object construction, adding ceremony without real stabilization.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx has broad dependency-heavy memo blocks and repeated object spreading for handoff objects, increasing local cognitive load for routine edits.

`buildTravelRequestBody` accepts both grouped objects (`motionParams`, `generationParams`, `seedParams`) and many legacy flat fields in the same signature, then resolves precedence internally (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:463-521`).

Task/request types still expose overlapping legacy and new fields simultaneously (`structure_guidance` and `structure_videos`, plus `LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`) in one API contract (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts:249-335`).

UI prop APIs expose very wide flattened callback/state surfaces and then collapse groups with spread-based resolvers, increasing key collision and naming drift risk (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:161-180`, `src/shared/components/JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts:95-112`).

Generation invalidation helpers with similar names have divergent behavior contracts (debounced/cancelable hook callback, fire-and-forget timeout path, and awaited async refetch path) (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:58-141`).
`src/shared/hooks/useShotCreation.ts` contains heavy banner/comment scaffolding and very broad inline type declarations that restate behavior and add cognitive overhead.
`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx` includes long explanatory comment blocks around straightforward hook wiring and prop forwarding.
Large orchestration modules combine comment-heavy ceremony with thin wrapper layers, a recurring generated-style signal in this batch.
src/types/shots.ts is marked deprecated with a sunset contract (remove by 2026-05-31) but remains an active compatibility barrel export.
src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts still imports GenerationRow from '@/types/shots', demonstrating ongoing use of the deprecated import path.

src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts keeps many deprecated legacy fields on ProjectImageSettings (selectedReferenceId/styleReference*/subject*), extending dual-schema support in the main type surface.

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts defines a legacy shim with deprecated task fields and a remove-by date, but no enforcement mechanism in this module to prevent indefinite retention.
holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles['src/shared/hooks/'] shows 98 production files, far above the >10 mixed-concern overload threshold for flat directories.
holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles['src/shared/components/'] shows 80 files (60 production + 20 tests), indicating another broad mixed-responsibility bucket.

Assigned files in these roots are concern-diverse: AI prompt orchestration (`useAIInteractionService.ts`), gallery prefetch/cache behavior (`useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts`), credential persistence (`useApiKeys.ts`), and UI rendering (`ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`) all co-located under generic `shared/*` roots.

holistic_context.structure.coupling_matrix includes reciprocal high-volume edges between `src/shared/components/` and `src/shared/hooks/` (`components -> hooks: 58` and reverse via directory imported_by), which signals blurred directory boundaries rather than clear package seams.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` combines timeline dimensions, drag/drop, zoom, selection, pending frame state, endpoint drag, video-browser insertion, and prefetch concerns in one 600+ line hook and returns a very wide surface.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/TimelineContainer/TimelineContainer.tsx` destructures dozens of values/handlers from `useTimelineOrchestrator`, indicating a low-cohesion orchestration contract rather than focused seams.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` is split into helper hooks but still concentrates environment setup, shared-state wiring, edit orchestration, panel selection, workflow props mapping, and rendering in one module.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` builds a large context object across multiple edit subsystems and includes placeholder/no-op wiring (`isFlippedHorizontally = false`, `isSaving = false`, `handleExitInpaintMode: () => {}`), which signals abstraction mismatch.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useGenerationController.ts`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/controllers/useEditingController.ts`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/ShotEditorLayout.tsx` all use very large param/return prop bags, showing responsibility mixing and weak boundaries.

`src/shared/components/TasksPane/TasksPane.tsx` and `src/shared/components/GenerationsPane/GenerationsPane.tsx` duplicate pane-orchestration concerns (locking/open sync, delayed pointer-events gating, backdrop close handling) rather than sharing a common pane behavior layer.

UI primitives use a distinct formatting convention (double quotes, no semicolons) in `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx:1`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx:1`, and `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx:1`, while most neighboring app files use single quotes/semicolons.
A separate local style drift appears in routing imports (`src/app/routes.tsx:22`) where one import switches quote style inside an otherwise single-quote file.

Centralized error normalization exists (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:24`), but several paths bypass it with direct `toast.error` and early returns in `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/MyLorasTab/hooks/useLoraSubmission.ts:161` and `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/MyLorasTab/hooks/useLoraSubmission.ts:181`.

Other paths log raw console errors (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:57`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:63`) while sibling paths use `handleError`.

Task batch flow uses direct `console.error` in dev (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:508`) plus `handleError`/rethrow later (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:519`), creating mixed failure reporting contracts.

Auth redirect is applied on `/` via `authRedirectLoader` (`src/app/routes.tsx:50`, `src/app/routes.tsx:60`) but not on sibling `/home` (`src/app/routes.tsx:66`), even though both render the same page.

The redirect decision is based on localStorage token presence (`src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts:65`) rather than validated session freshness; comments explicitly defer authoritative checks to `Layout` (`src/app/routes.tsx:48`).
authorization_consistency.unreported_risk: If route-level auth handling continues to split between optimistic token checks and later authoritative checks, additional entry routes can drift and produce inconsistent signed-in/signed-out UX.

findings.summary: Central error utility is coupled to UI toast rendering across non-UI modules.
evidence: `handleError` imports `toast` directly and conditionally renders UI (`toast({...})`) in `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts`.
Non-UI hooks call `handleError(...)` for control-path failures, so importing error handling also imports UI behavior into shared/domain flows.
findings.suggestion: Split normalization/logging from presentation: keep a pure `handleErrorCore` in shared lib (normalize + log + metadata), and move toast emission to UI-facing adapters/hooks so callers opt in explicitly to presentation side effects.

findings.summary: Supabase client module still performs runtime side effects at import time.
evidence: Module comment explicitly states eager creation at import boundary, and code exports `supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient()`.
Initialization path installs instrumentation, writes window globals, and calls `initializeSupabaseRuntime(client)` during module load.
findings.suggestion: Replace eager export with a lazy accessor (`getSupabaseClient`) and invoke initialization from explicit app bootstrap points; keep diagnostics/global registration behind debug flags or dedicated dev bootstrap to reduce implicit import-time behavior.

findings.summary: Deprecated type shim remains on active import paths, prolonging boundary ambiguity.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` is marked deprecated but still re-exports all of `@/types/generationAndShots`.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` imports `Shot` from `@/types/shots`, keeping callers on the compatibility alias instead of the target module.
findings.suggestion: Migrate assigned-scope imports to `@/types/generationAndShots` and narrow `src/types/shots.ts` to explicit temporary aliases (or remove it once callers are migrated) to restore a single authoritative type boundary.

findings.summary: Mobile/touch interaction policy is spread across unrelated shared modules.
evidence: `useMobile.tsx` defines device heuristics and multiple reactive hooks; `tooltip.tsx` adds separate touch-toggle/outside-close logic with document listeners.
`useMobileInteractions.ts` implements independent double-tap and outside-click behavior; `LightboxShell.tsx` adds another pointer/touch policy layer with z-index gating and global listeners.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared interaction policy layer (for example `shared/lib/interactionPolicy`) that centralizes pointer/touch classification and outside-interaction semantics, then have tooltip/gallery/lightbox consume that contract instead of each module maintaining its own event rules.

findings.summary: Image edit state assembly uses oversized pass-through interfaces with low abstraction leverage.
evidence: `UseImageEditValueParams` enumerates dozens of fields, and `buildModeState`/`buildCanvasAndAnnotationState`/`buildFormAndGenerationState` mostly copy them verbatim.
`useInlineEditState` builds `buildImageEditValueParams(...)` from multiple sub-hooks, then returns another nested object tree that re-exposes the same values.
Callers like `InlineEditView` consume deeply nested state groups, indicating the abstraction is moving data shapes around more than hiding behavior.
findings.suggestion: Collapse `useImageEditValue` and `useInlineEditState` around a smaller domain model: keep behavior-centric hooks (inpaint/reposition/img2img) separate and pass those sub-objects directly through context, only adapting keys where type translation is truly needed.

findings.summary: Button group prop builders duplicate shared context and add indirection without transformation.
evidence: `buildTopLeftProps`, `buildTopRightProps`, `buildBottomLeftProps`, and `buildBottomRightProps` all re-copy the same shared fields.
Both image and video lightbox render flows then re-spread and override `buttonGroupProps.topRight` (`handleDownload`, `handleDelete`) before passing to `useLightboxWorkflowProps`.
Most fields are forwarded unchanged, so the abstraction layer adds call depth rather than reducing boundary complexity.
findings.suggestion: Replace per-corner builder fan-out with a single `sharedControls` object plus narrow per-corner deltas, and let `useLightboxWorkflowProps` accept explicit overrides to avoid spread-and-rewrite chains in both image/video render models.

findings.summary: DB-to-domain task mapping is duplicated across modules, creating seam drift risk.
evidence: `useTasks.ts` exports `mapDbTaskToTask` with a local `TaskDbRow` type.
`generationTaskBridge.ts` defines another `TaskDbRow` and `mapDbTaskRowToTask` with the same conversion logic.
Both modules perform direct task-row translation, so schema updates must be synchronized manually across multiple seams.
findings.suggestion: Promote a single shared mapper/type in one task-domain module (for example under `src/shared/lib/tasks/`) and import it from both hooks and bridge utilities to keep translation policy centralized.

findings.summary: Data freshness policy class is coupled to toast UI, blurring integration boundaries.
evidence: `DataFreshnessManager` imports `toast` and emits user-facing errors inside `onFetchFailure`.
`useTasks` invokes `dataFreshnessManager.onFetchFailure(...)` in query functions, so data-layer failures automatically trigger presentation-layer behavior.
This makes polling/circuit-breaker logic harder to reuse or test independently from UI notification policy.
findings.suggestion: Refactor `DataFreshnessManager` to emit typed failure events/status only, and move toast decisions to a UI subscriber (hook/provider) that listens to manager state changes.

findings.summary: Multiple hooks use `useMemo` with whole-object deps, adding complexity with little benefit.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` memoizes output with dependency `[params]`, which usually changes identity every render.
`useImageEditValue` uses `useMemo(..., [params])` around pure object construction from a large param bag.
Both patterns keep memoization scaffolding while typically recomputing anyway, obscuring simple data assembly paths.
findings.suggestion: Either remove `useMemo` for these pure cheap builders, or switch to stable primitive/function dependencies (or upstream memoized inputs) so memoization semantics match actual render behavior.

findings.summary: Travel request builder exposes two overlapping parameter models in one public function.
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBody` accepts both structured groups (`motionParams`, `generationParams`, `seedParams`) and deprecated flat fields, then computes fallback precedence internally (`generateVideoService.ts:463-521`).
The task type also composes `LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` with new fields, keeping mixed-era keys in one contract (`types.ts:249-335`).
findings.suggestion: Split into explicit APIs: a single canonical request-builder input shape for production calls, and move legacy/test compatibility into a separate adapter (`fromLegacyTravelParams`) with deprecation boundary.

findings.summary: Legacy single-video and new multi-video structure APIs coexist and overlap across boundaries.
evidence: Shot editor props still expose legacy `primaryStructureVideo*` fields alongside `structureVideos` array and separate callbacks (`ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:76-87`, `:120-135`).
Task params advertise both unified `structure_guidance` and separate `structure_videos`, while also inheriting legacy structure fields (`tasks/.../types.ts:249-324`).
Service only derives guidance from `structureVideos` first-item semantics (`generateVideoService.ts:80-121`), making mixed callers hard to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Finalize one structure-guidance contract end-to-end: remove legacy single-video props from component APIs, keep one normalized structure input in service, and enforce it in `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`.

findings.summary: Spread-based prop resolvers flatten large grouped APIs into collision-prone wide surfaces.
evidence: `resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps` merges three groups via object spread into a single resolved type (`JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts:101-112`).
`resolveShotImagesEditorProps` merges four large groups similarly (`ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:168-180`), including many optional callbacks and similarly named fields.
findings.suggestion: Stop flattening grouped props at type boundary; keep grouped namespaces (`clipSettings`, `motionConfig`, etc.) through component internals or use explicit prefixed remaps where flattening is required.

findings.summary: Generation invalidation seam has inconsistent lifecycle semantics across related entry points.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` coalesces per-shot delayed invalidations with cancellable timeout refs (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:60-77`).
`invalidateGenerationsSync` uses `setTimeout` without cancellation/cleanup and returns void (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84-97`).

`invalidateVariantChange` is async, awaits delays, and mixes `invalidateQueries` with awaited `refetchQueries` (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:105-141`), unlike the explicit queue/event lifecycle in timeline writes (`timelineWriteQueue.ts:108-190`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce one invalidation orchestrator with a single async contract (supports optional delay, cancellation token, and refetch policy), then make hook/sync helpers thin adapters around that contract.

findings.summary: Auth/session/settings boundary mixes silent-null, wrapped success objects, and thrown errors.
evidence: `readRawSession` swallows parse/storage failures and returns `null` (`supabaseSession.ts:28-36`).
`getUserWithTimeout` always resolves a Supabase-like success shape and never actually times out (`toolSettingsService.ts:84-100`).
`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` then throws `Error`, rethrows `DOMException`, and conditionally returns defaults based on string matching (`toolSettingsService.ts:220-243`, `:232-239`).
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit `Result`-style auth/settings boundary contract (e.g., `ok | unauthenticated | cancelled | network_error`) and remove message-string branching plus mixed throw/null semantics.

findings.summary: Deprecated `@/types/shots` bridge remains in active use across current code paths.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` is a pure deprecated re-export with a sunset contract (`Remove by: 2026-05-31`).
`src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts` line 5 still imports `GenerationRow` from `@/types/shots`, keeping the deprecated path alive.
findings.suggestion: Migrate remaining imports to `@/types/generationAndShots` directly, add a temporary lint guard banning new `@/types/shots` imports, then remove `src/types/shots.ts` once call sites are exhausted.

findings.summary: Legacy generation/task schema fields are still embedded in primary shared types.
evidence: `ProjectImageSettings` in `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` keeps multiple `@deprecated` fields in the core interface, extending mixed old/new settings shapes.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` preserves deprecated payload fields behind a shim and date comment, but this module itself does not enforce expiry.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy fields into explicit `Legacy*` adapter types used only in migration/parsing boundaries; keep modern interfaces clean and add a runtime/CI assertion keyed to the remove-by date to block continued legacy-field usage.

findings.summary: Several utility files carry high-volume explanatory prose that largely restates obvious code.
evidence: `image-utils.ts` includes long narrative comments about ID semantics and timeline positioning around short, direct helper functions.
`storagePaths.ts` documents each trivial template-literal path helper with repetitive prose/examples, creating high comment-to-logic ratio.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to non-obvious invariants and migration constraints only; remove repetitive line-by-line restatements so maintenance effort stays on behavior rather than duplicated prose.

findings.summary: Aspect-ratio fallback behavior is duplicated across two helpers with overlapping responsibilities.
evidence: `getAspectRatioStyle` and `getImageAspectRatioStyle` both parse project aspect ratio via `parseRatio` and both default to `'1'` when missing/invalid.
This duplication creates local elegance debt and raises risk of subtle divergence in fallback behavior.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `resolveAspectRatio` helper (inputs: optional width/height, optional project ratio) and call it from both files so fallback semantics are defined once.

findings.summary: Large flat shared directories mix unrelated domains and obscure ownership boundaries.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/` contains 98 production files in one directory profile, while the assigned hooks cover unrelated concerns (AI orchestration, pagination preloading, API-key persistence).
`src/shared/components/` contains 80 files with heterogeneous responsibilities; `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` and `AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx` are in the same broad root despite unrelated domains.
High cross-coupling between shared components and hooks (58 edge count) indicates broad, non-specific package seams.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders by concern: `src/shared/hooks/ai/`, `src/shared/hooks/preloading/`, `src/shared/hooks/integrations/`, and move corresponding tests under matching `__tests__` subfolders; (2) move in low-blast-radius order: `useAdjacentPagePreloader*` first, then `useAIInteractionService*`, then `useApiKeys*`; (3) run import updates with `desloppify move src/shared/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts src/shared/hooks/preloading/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts --dry-run` (repeat for each file, then apply) and verify no stale paths via `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/(useAdjacentPagePreloader|useAIInteractionService|useApiKeys)" src`; (4) validate with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAdjacentPagePreloader.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAIInteractionService.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useApiKeys.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Feature-specific logic is placed in shared packages, weakening top-level domain decomposition.
evidence: `useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` includes MediaGallery-specific intent in comments and behavior but sits in a global shared hook package.
`useApiKeys.ts` contains direct Supabase persistence/auth policy decisions, which are data-access ownership concerns rather than generic UI hook concerns.
`useAIInteractionService.ts` centralizes prompt-generation/edit RPC behavior tied to image/prompt flows, yet is not grouped with that feature boundary.
These files together indicate top-level package names (`shared/hooks`) are not mapping to stable domain capabilities.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) define domain-first targets: `src/features/media-gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts`, `src/features/image-generation/hooks/useAIInteractionService.ts`, `src/shared/data/user-api-keys/useApiKeys.ts`; (2) move hooks first, then adjust nearest consumers (`MediaGalleryGrid`, prompt/image-gen modules), then relocate tests alongside new domains; (3) perform import rewrites with `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run` followed by actual move, then confirm with `rg -n "useAdjacentPagePreloader|useAIInteractionService|useApiKeys" src`; (4) validate architecture and behavior with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx vitest run src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAIInteractionService.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAdjacentPagePreloader.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useApiKeys.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Timeline orchestration hook owns too many unrelated behaviors behind one wide contract
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` (664 lines) manages zoom, drag, drop interception, selection, pending states, endpoint dragging, video browser insertion, and computed pair data in one place.
`TimelineContainer` performs a very large destructure from the hook, coupling UI rendering directly to a broad internal state machine and many imperative handlers.
findings.suggestion: Split `useTimelineOrchestrator` into domain-specific controller hooks with narrow return types (for example: `useTimelineInteraction`, `useTimelineViewport`, `useTimelineInsertion`, `useTimelineSelection`) and expose a small facade object consumed by `TimelineContainer`.

findings.summary: Image lightbox edit stack still leaks cross-mode state and placeholder wiring
evidence: `ImageLightbox.tsx` still centralizes environment creation, shared state, edit model wiring, control panel branching, workflow prop mapping, and final composition despite prior extraction.

`useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` composes multiple mode hooks into one large context payload and includes hardcoded placeholder/no-op fields (`isFlippedHorizontally = false`, `isSaving = false`, `handleExitInpaintMode: () => {}`), indicating incomplete boundary ownership.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit per-mode context slices (`inpaint`, `magicEdit`, `reposition`, `img2img`) with typed adapters and remove placeholder/no-op fields by either implementing real state ownership or deleting unsupported contract fields.

findings.summary: Shot editor controllers and layout rely on oversized param/return bags
evidence: `useGenerationController` takes a long mixed-responsibility parameter list (generation config, join settings, UI loading flags, and setter callbacks), then forwards many unchanged values into `useGenerateBatch`.

`useEditingController` and `ShotEditorLayout` expose similarly broad interfaces spanning unrelated areas (structure video, audio, naming, join config, modal state), creating high coupling and fragile call sites.
findings.suggestion: Refactor controller/layout contracts into grouped domain objects (`generation`, `join`, `structureVideo`, `uiState`, `actions`) and pass those as stable typed modules rather than flat bags; then co-locate each domain’s state+actions with its consuming section.

findings.summary: Sliding pane behavior is duplicated between tasks and generations panes
evidence: Both pane components independently implement similar orchestration around `useSlidingPane`: lock/open synchronization to context, delayed pointer-event enablement after animation, mobile backdrop close handlers, and pane-control wiring.
This parallel logic has already drifted (different z-index/mobile interaction details), making behavior consistency and future changes harder.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared pane-shell abstraction (hook or component) that owns lock/open syncing, delayed interaction gating, and backdrop semantics; keep each pane focused on domain content and pane-specific controls.

findings.summary: Related flows mix `handleError`, direct toasts, and console logs, fragmenting error contracts.
evidence: `handleError` is the centralized normalize/log/toast path (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:24`), but LoRA upload failures use `toast.error` + `return null` (`src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/MyLorasTab/hooks/useLoraSubmission.ts:161`, `:181`) without normalization.
Gallery notifier failures log via `console.error` (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:57`, `:63`) instead of a consistent app error contract.
Batch generation logs per-task failures to console (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:508`) while outer failures also call `handleError` (`:519`), yielding mixed observability paths.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on `handleError` (or `handleAndRethrow`) for all caught operational failures; wrap direct toast/console branches in a shared adapter so each failure path emits normalized context and optional user toast from one contract.

findings.summary: Two routes serving HomePage apply different auth redirect behavior.
evidence: `/` uses `loader: authRedirectLoader` (`src/app/routes.tsx:60`) which redirects when `hasStoredSession()` is true (`src/app/routes.tsx:51`).
`/home` renders the same `HomePage` (`src/app/routes.tsx:67`) but has no equivalent redirect loader.
The loader relies on `hasStoredSessionToken()` (`src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts:65`), so auth flow differs by entry route and by token-presence heuristic.
findings.suggestion: Apply one shared loader policy to both `/` and `/home` (either both redirect-if-session or both remain neutral) and keep token-presence checks explicitly scoped as a UX optimization, not route-specific auth behavior.

findings.summary: UI primitive files follow a distinct code-style island that drifts from surrounding conventions.
evidence: Multiple UI primitives use double-quote/no-semicolon formatting (`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx:1`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx:1`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx:1`) unlike most reviewed files.
Additional quote-style drift appears inside non-UI files (`src/app/routes.tsx:22`) in otherwise single-quote code.
findings.suggestion: Define formatter/lint ownership for vendored UI primitives vs app-owned code (for example, isolate generated/vendor-style files or normalize all files with one formatter profile) to remove recurring style outliers.

findings.summary: Supabase client import triggers runtime wiring and global state mutation.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` eagerly constructs `supabase` at import time and initializes runtime instrumentation/auth reconnect wiring.
The module also writes `window.__supabase_client__` and `window.supabase`, creating mutable global coupling.
findings.suggestion: Replace eager singleton export with `getSupabaseClient()` lazy accessor and move runtime initialization behind explicit app bootstrap (`src/app/bootstrap.tsx`) so startup side effects occur in one controlled place.

findings.summary: Shot creation flows mix throw, null-return, and silent handling semantics.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useShotCreation.ts` throws internally but returns `null` from top-level catch.
`src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotCreation.ts` throws in mutation paths but returns `null` for external drop failures.
`src/app/hooks/useAppExternalDrop.ts` catches and suppresses toast (`showToast: false`) for drag-drop failures.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one domain error contract for shot creation (e.g., `Result` object with `ok/error`) and update all three boundaries to propagate typed failures consistently, with centralized UX mapping.

findings.summary: MediaLightbox abstractions add indirection while preserving very wide interfaces.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` is large and then re-bundled in `useBundledLightboxProps` into grouped objects without significant boundary reduction.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` creates multiple intermediate memoized wrappers that mostly forward existing values.
findings.suggestion: Split the boundary into smaller role-specific components (`ImageLightboxShell`, `VideoLightboxShell`, `SegmentSlotShell`) with narrower typed props and remove pass-through provider/render-model wrappers that do not enforce policy.

findings.summary: Two different public hooks share `useShotCreation` name with incompatible contracts.
evidence: A top-level unified hook and a shots-submodule composite hook both expose `useShotCreation` semantics under different import paths.
The hooks differ in return/error behavior and intended abstraction level.
findings.suggestion: Rename one API to intent-specific naming (`useUnifiedShotCreation` vs `useShotCreationMutations`) and enforce a single canonical import path via lint rule/codemod.

findings.summary: Large comment/type ceremony obscures core behavior in orchestration files.
evidence: `useShotCreation.ts` contains extensive banner comments and many local interface declarations around relatively standard orchestration logic.
`MediaGallery/index.tsx` includes long explanatory blocks and repeated framing comments around direct hook composition.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to rationale-only notes, move reusable domain types to dedicated `types.ts` files, and keep implementation files focused on executable behavior.

findings.summary: Deprecated shot types and legacy migration code remain in active runtime paths.
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` is marked deprecated with a removal date but is still imported by active feature files in this batch.
`useLegacyMigrations` runs several legacy transformations every render cycle where inputs match old structures.
`segmentSettingsMigration.ts` retains deprecated and legacy metadata shape support alongside new shape support.
findings.suggestion: Define a migration cutoff plan: backfill persisted data once, introduce runtime telemetry for legacy-shape reads, then remove `@/types/shots` and legacy branches behind a dated feature flag removal sequence.

findings.summary: Shared directories are oversized, mixed-concern hubs with high cross-coupling.
evidence: Packet structure metrics: `src/shared/components/` has 80 files and `src/shared/hooks/` has 98 files, both above the >10 mixed-concern threshold.
Coupling matrix shows strong horizontal dependencies from shared hooks to infrastructure (`src/shared/hooks/ -> src/integrations/supabase/` = 50 edges; `-> src/shared/lib/errorHandling/` = 44).
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/shared/features/media/`, `src/shared/features/shots/`, `src/shared/features/settings/`, `src/shared/infrastructure/supabase/`. Stage 2 move highest-affinity modules first (MediaLightbox/MediaGallery* to `features/media`, shot creation/mutations to `features/shots`, settings/token hooks to `features/settings`). Stage 3 collapse pass-through root files into feature entrypoints and keep `src/shared/components/` for true cross-feature primitives only. Run import updates with `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run` then apply, validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm -s test`, and `rg "@/shared/components/|@/shared/hooks/" src` to verify residual stragglers.

findings.summary: Top-level shared component ownership is blurred by catch-all placement.
evidence: A single top-level directory mixes domain UI, wrappers, and migration/util modules, obscuring capability boundaries.
Files like `segmentSettingsMigration.ts` live beside UI components, signaling ownership drift between domain logic and presentation.
findings.suggestion: Reframe shared layer by capability ownership: place migration/domain logic under feature-domain folders and reserve top-level shared UI for reusable primitives only.

findings.summary: Media workflows rely on seam-heavy choreography across many intermediate models.
evidence: `MediaGallery/index.tsx` composes many hooks and propagates grouped state/actions through layered boundaries.
`MediaGalleryItem.tsx` orchestrates shot/task/share/navigation concerns in one seam point before forwarding to subcomponents.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` translates multiple models into another state model, increasing handoff complexity.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit seam adapters per concern (`task seam`, `shot seam`, `share seam`) and pass minimal typed contracts into view components instead of wide grouped objects.

findings.summary: Single effect in home auth hook mixes too many responsibilities and deep nesting.
evidence: `useHomeAuth.ts` effect handles hash parsing, session bootstrapping, standalone mode redirects, auth subscriptions, referral RPC side effects, and storage cleanup in one body.
Nested async and repeated `try/catch` blocks make branch behavior hard to test and reason about locally.
findings.suggestion: Split `useHomeAuth` into focused internal helpers/hooks (`useOAuthHashSessionRestore`, `useStandaloneRedirect`, `useReferralLinkingOnSignIn`, `useHomeAuthSubscription`) and keep the top-level effect as ordered composition only.
